text: Machine learning is a type of artificial intelligence where computers learn from data instead of being explicitly programmed. One common approach is supervised learning, which uses labelled training data. This data contains examples with correct answers, like photos labelled "cat" or "dog." The computer uses this data to build a model, which is a set of rules or patterns it discovers. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new, unseen data. For example, it might predict whether a new photo shows a cat or a dog. The model's performance is measured by its accuracy, or how often its predictions are correct. More high-quality training data usually leads to better accuracy.
